purpose: Classifies financial structures by fragility to identify systemic credit risk accumulation. Answers: where is hidden leverage and what triggers the transition from stability to instability?
components: Hedge finance: income covers principal and interest (hyperscaler AI capex)
Speculative finance: income covers interest but not principal (data center REITs at 5-7x)
Ponzi finance: income covers neither — requires asset price appreciation (GPU leasing, AI venture debt)
Endogenous transition mechanism: stability breeds complacency breeds fragility
current_assessment: The credit ecosystem shows bifurcation: hedge-financed mega-cap core vs. increasingly speculative/Ponzi downstream. EBITDA addbacks mask the true Minsky classification by overstating income coverage. Covenant-lite structures delay the recognition of Ponzi transitions. The maturity wall (2025-2027) is the likely forcing function for reclassification.
limitations: Downstream AI Ponzi quantification is too vague to be actionable (self-assessed 5/10)
Without bottom-up aggregation, aggregate systemic interconnection is unknown
The scale of the current covenant-lite cycle is unprecedented, making step-function predictions untested at this scale
Private credit opacity prevents accurate real-time Minsky classification
